name: Faithful Saints of Christ Book
longDescription: Faithful Saints of Christ is both a spiritual journey and a powerful historical testimony. It traces the miracles, struggles, and perseverance that shaped Armenian history. Readers will witness the courage of Armenians who defended their Christian faith against oppression—with emphasis on the story of St. Vartan and the Battle of Avarayr: the conflict in which the Armenians decisively confirmed their identity as a Christian people. The narrative continues into modern history, recounting the Armenian Genocide of 1915 in Turkey, the hidden genocide of 1918 in Iran, and the ongoing persecution of Christians worldwide. Faithful Saints of Christ honors the saints of yesterday who remained steadfast under persecution, while encouraging believers of today to stay faithful to Christ against the obstacles of the present world.
authors: Christopher H. Zakian (Director of Communications for the Eastern Diocese of the Armenian Church of America)
Dr Michael Papazian (Professor of Religion and Philosophy at Berry College)
Dr Rosemary Hartounian Cohen (Sociologist with a doctorate degree from the Sorbonne, Paris)
Armen Simonian (Founder of First Christians Alliance Outreach)

name: Pre-Battle Scene of Avarayr Portrait
shortDescription: Reproduction copy of original oil painting masterpiece by famous French artist, Mathieu Nozieres. On canvas, without frame.
longDescription: The Pre-Battle Scene of Avarayr Portrait is a reproduction copy of the original oil painting masterpiece by famous French Artist, Mathieu Nozieres. The oil painting was produced as a complement to the Faithful Saints of Christ book, and a cropped portion of the painting is presented as the cover of the book. The events surrounding the Battle of Avarayr are described in detail in the book. High-quality reproduction copies are available for purchase. The original oil painting masterpiece, which is 30 inches high and 60 inches wide, has been appraised for $100,000 by a prominent art appraiser.
